```python
# Signed: Claude + claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022, 2024-10-22
# Format: MurphySig v0.3.3 (https://murphysig.dev/spec)
#
# Context: A simple slugify function for converting strings to URL-safe slugs
#
# Confidence: 0.9 - The regex pattern handles basic cases but may need adjustment for Unicode characters
# Open: Should this support Unicode characters? Should leading/trailing hyphens be handled differently?

def slugify(text: str) -> str:
    """Convert a string to a URL-safe slug."""
    import re
    text = text.lower().strip()
    text = re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9]+", "-", text)
    text = text.strip("-")
    return text
```
